name: Fathom Analytics API
what_it_does: Privacy-first web analytics API that provides GDPR-compliant pageview data, event tracking, and real-time visitor statistics without cookies or personal data collection.
use_cases: Querying website traffic and pageview data for reporting agents
Monitoring real-time active visitor counts for capacity planning
Tracking custom events (sign-ups, purchases) and conversion funnels via API
Generating traffic reports and trend analysis without data residency concerns
Comparing performance of multiple sites from a single Fathom account
not_for: Session replay or heatmap analysis (use FullStory/Hotjar instead)
Ecommerce analytics requiring cart abandonment or product funnel depth
best_when: An agent needs to pull website analytics for privacy-conscious products without the GDPR complexity of Google Analytics.
avoid_when: You need deep behavioral analytics, A/B testing, or funnel analysis with user-level granularity.

auth.notes: Single API token from account settings. No scope granularity — token grants full account read access. HTTPS required for all requests.

pricing.paid_tiers: From $15/month for 100k pageviews, scaling up by traffic volume
pricing.notes: Priced around pageview volume. One of the more affordable privacy-first analytics options. Unlimited sites on all plans.

performance.rate_limits: Not publicly documented; practical limit is reasonable request rates

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: API documentation is concise and accurate for the available endpoints. Error responses are JSON with message fields. Limited endpoint surface makes the API easy to use.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Read-only API — no write operations. All requests are idempotent by nature.
known_agent_gotchas: API is read-only — you cannot create sites or configure tracking via API
Date ranges must be specified in UTC; Fathom stores data in UTC
Data aggregation is by day at minimum; real-time endpoint shows current visitors only
No webhook support — agents must poll for new data

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Single-token model with no scopes is a limitation for multi-agent use. Privacy-by-design architecture means no PII stored, reducing data breach risk.
